repo_prior_art.cite: none; the PartialID catalogue contains Manski mean-bound work, not generalized-Roy common-law pasting

published_prior_art.cite: fan_guerre_zhu_2017_joint_po_functionals treats joint-potential-outcome functionals, but not this finite generalized-Roy response-array pasting construction

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue entry studies an IV/OT gain-band association-loss sign frontier

published_prior_art.cite: tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot covers generalized-Roy product-cost PRTE OT; masten_poirier_2020_breakdown_frontiers is the closest sensitivity-frontier antecedent
tan_blanchet_syrgkanis_2026_prte_ot supplies the separable product-cost regime nested by this proposition

per_theorem_calls.label: Open-ended Question 1 (orthogonal inference for sign radius)

published_prior_art.cite: ober_reynolds_2023_joint_po_ot gives dual-OT root-n endpoint inference and fang_santos_2019_directional_inference gives the directional framework; generated generalized-Roy cutpoints remain a distinct combination

structure_flags.label: Theorem 1
structure_flags.one_line: The strongest open kernel is labelled a theorem although it remains to prove; relabel it a conjecture or make the OEQ the headline.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 / related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest breakdown-frontier antecedent is Masten and Poirier (2020), Inference on Breakdown Frontiers, Quantitative Economics 11:41-111, but the proposal instead cites their different 2018 conditional-partial-independence paper.

novelty_flags.label: Bibliography / masten_poirier_2018_breakdown_frontiers
novelty_flags.one_line: The cited 2018 Econometrica paper is Identification of Treatment Effects Under Conditional Partial Independence, not the breakdown-frontier framework invoked in the theorem gap; the relevant paper is Masten and Poirier (2020), QE1288.

novelty_flags.label: Bibliography / gao_ge_qian_2025_causal_mot
novelty_flags.one_line: The AISTATS 2025 paper is by Zijun Gao, Shu Ge, and Jian Qian, not Ruocheng Gao, Yuxuan Ge, and Yao Qian.

novelty_flags.label: Bibliography / han_shenshen_yang_policy_iv
novelty_flags.one_line: Han and Yang's cited work was published as a 2024 Journal of Econometrics article, not a 2024 working paper.

novelty_flags.label: Bibliography / marx_2024_sharp_bounds_latent_index
novelty_flags.one_line: Marx (2024) is Journal of Econometrics 238(2), 105561, not 240(1), 105678.

novelty_flags.label: Bibliography / frandsen_lefgren_2021_dte_kipp
novelty_flags.one_line: Frandsen and Lefgren (2021) is Quantitative Economics 12(1), 143-171, not pages 901-939.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 1 / technical_internal_limitation
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Finite grids, saturated cutpoint coverage, both rank-shape restrictions, and the calibrated association floor are maintained rather than derived.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Sharp only under the displayed finite-grid generalized-Roy rank-association sensitivity model.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Root-n inference, cutpoint stability, and tie-robust directional inference are not claimed by the identification theorem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The identification claim is conditional and contains no inference guarantee; inference remains an OEQ.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Relabel the headline theorem as a conjecture and state the exact finite-grid generalized-Roy model in that headline.

Replace the incorrect Masten citation with Masten and Poirier (2020), then distinguish the scalar association-loss radius from their rank-invariance breakdown frontier through a named observable-law comparison.

Demonstrate a strict nonseparable gain-band advance over the Tan--Blanchet--Syrgkanis product-cost reduction on a specified observable-law family, rather than presenting the parametric-LP frontier alone as the novelty.
Correct the Gao, Han--Yang, Marx, and Frandsen--Lefgren bibliography metadata before the next literature check.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 for relabelling, citation repair, and a sharper comparator-based account of why the finite common-law sign frontier is more than a routine parametric-LP re-expression.
